fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form lists ICD-10 code M19.011, the clinical notes list M19.011, and the prescription lists M19.011. The codes are consistent across all three documents; however, a clinical-documentation conflict exists: the medical justification narrative across the clinical notes and prescription references 'safe ambulation and prevent falls' and an inability to 'navigate living space without assistive device,' which is more consistent with a mobility/gait disorder rather than isolated right shoulder osteoarthritis. Additionally, a hospital bed (E0250) is not typically indicated for a shoulder osteoarthritis diagnosis alone — it is more commonly justified by a condition affecting mobility or positioning. The diagnosis code itself is consistent across all documents, so M19.011 is retained as the resolved code, but the mismatch between diagnosis, equipment ordered, and justification language warrants escalation for clinical review.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis vs. Equipment/Justification Alignment
icd_conflicts.reasoning: While the ICD-10 code M19.011 (primary osteoarthritis, right shoulder) is identical across all three documents, the medical justification language ('safe ambulation,' 'prevent falls,' 'unable to safely navigate living space') and the equipment ordered (hospital bed, E0250) are clinically inconsistent with a shoulder osteoarthritis diagnosis, suggesting a possible documentation error or missing secondary diagnosis code; escalation is required for physician clarification.

fields.priority_reason: Post-surgical case with active wound healing, escalation required due to ICD-diagnosis-to-equipment mismatch and low confidence (62) in clinical justification alignment, requiring urgent physician clarification before fulfillment.
fields.notes: 1. Physician NPI is blank on all three documents — must be obtained before processing. 2. Authorization Reference Number is missing — adjuster Sarah Mitchell should be contacted at (312) 555-0177 or s.mitchell@travelers.com to obtain prior authorization before delivery. 3. The medical necessity justification ('safe ambulation,' 'prevent falls') does not align with a shoulder osteoarthritis diagnosis; a hospital bed is not standard DME for M19.011 and may indicate a documentation error, a missing secondary mobility-related diagnosis, or the wrong HCPCS code was selected. 4. Patient height is 6'4" / 157 lbs — if a hospital bed is confirmed appropriate, a standard E0250 frame should be verified for height accommodation. 5. The referral form lists the case manager as Sarah Mitchell, who is also the insurance adjuster — this dual role should be noted for compliance purposes. 6. Prescription is valid through approximately 11/25/2026 (90 days from 08/27/2026).

fired_rules.message: Laterality conflict detected: M19.011 vs M19.011 — Laterality mismatch across documents. Per §I.B.13, the treating physician clinical notes are authoritative — but conflicting documents require clinical review before dispatch.

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis vs. Equipment/Justification Alignment
